Corrections.PreviousClaim: The initial prototype poisoning vulnerability is mitigated because the conditional check 'if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_))' exists, and the `else` block uses `safe_string_copy` makes it a safe copy.
Corrections.CorrectedClaim: A stack buffer overflow SC-V3-005 exists because line 832 (`strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str());`) triggers when input length exceeds `config::limits::STACK_BUFFER_SIZE` (1024), resulting in a size mismatch. The reliance on `safe_string_copy` is noted but does not prevent the physical C-string overflow if the logic flow doesn't strictly enforce a hard cut before assignment when bounds are met.
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

truthAudit.Summary: The previous audit claimed robustness against typical buffer overflows/prototype poisoning due to introduced safe-string copying and runtime-level checks. Truth audit revealed that while conditional logic mitigates obvious flaws during safe-string copy, other vulnerabilities exist. Critically, SC-V3-005 (Stack Buffer Overflow) is present because of the combination of input handling and usage where `safe_string_copy` allocates size/does not check fully against `expression_buffer_` initialization or `config::limits::MAX_EXPRESSION_LENGTH` (which is 2048, potentially exceeded by expression length plus null terminator). Conversely, SC-V3-009 (Heap Overflow in Expression Parser) is triggered by `ExpressionParser::parse()` using oversized string formatting/copying, exacerbated by the way expression storage interacts with memory pools.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: it seems mitigated or accurately classified according to revised scrutiny involving safe_string_copy.
Items.Notes: SC-V3-005 details a Stack Buffer Overflow in Input Processing: SC-V3-005: Stack Buffer Overflow in Input Processing | severity=High | cwe=CWE-120/CWE-121 | anchors=strcpy(input_buffer_; dest[src_len]; src_len > dest_size
